name: Effia Nkwanta Regional Hospital
description: The largest public hospital in the Western Region, serving as the regional referral centre. Offers general medicine, surgery, maternity, paediatrics, and emergency services.

name: Axim Government Hospital
address: Axim, Nzema East Municipal District, Western Region
description: District-level government hospital serving Axim and surrounding coastal communities. Provides general outpatient care, maternity, and basic emergency services.

name: St. Martin's de Porres Hospital
description: Catholic mission hospital widely regarded as one of the best private health facilities in the Western Region, with strong maternal and surgical services.

name: Tarkwa Government Hospital
description: Government hospital serving the Tarkwa mining belt. Provides general medical, surgical, and maternity services for the municipality.

name: Kwesimintsim Government Hospital
description: Major public hospital in Takoradi metropolis, complementing Effia Nkwanta with primary and secondary care for the western suburbs.

name: Jubilee Hospital Takoradi
description: Modern private hospital in Takoradi offering specialist care including cardiology, orthopaedics, and diagnostic imaging.

name: University of Mines and Technology (UMaT)
description: Ghana's premier public university for mining, petroleum, and geological engineering. Established 2004 from the former Tarkwa School of Mines.

name: Takoradi Technical University (TTU)
description: Public technical university offering engineering, business, applied sciences, and hospitality programmes. Formerly Takoradi Polytechnic.

name: Fijai Senior High School
description: Co-educational public senior high school well known for strong science and mathematics performance in the Western Region.

name: Sekondi College
description: Historic boys' senior high school established 1947, with notable alumni in Ghanaian public life.

name: Takoradi Senior High School
description: Major co-educational senior high school in central Takoradi serving the metropolis.

name: Methodist Girls' Senior High School
description: All-girls Methodist senior high school in Tarkwa with strong academic record.

name: Ahantaman Senior High School
address: Agona Nkwanta, Ahanta West District, Western Region
description: Co-educational senior high school serving Ahanta West District communities.

name: Busua Beach
address: Busua, Ahanta West District, Western Region
description: One of Ghana's most popular beaches, with a long curve of soft white sand, calm surfable waves, and several beachfront lodges. About 30 km west of Takoradi.

name: Axim Beach
address: Axim Bay, Nzema East, Western Region
description: Wide palm-fringed beach overlooked by Fort St. Antonio. Less crowded than Busua, with strong surf — best for sunbathing and walking.

name: Butre Beach
address: Butre village, Ahanta West, Western Region
description: Quiet, scenic beach at the mouth of the Butre River, framed by the ruins of Fort Batenstein on the hill. A favourite for kayaking and birdwatching.

name: Cape Three Points Beach
address: Cape Three Points, Ahanta West, Western Region
description: The southernmost point of Ghana, marked by the Cape Three Points lighthouse. Dramatic Atlantic coastline with rocky headlands and undeveloped beaches.

name: Akwidaa Beach
description: Long, undeveloped white-sand beach near Cape Three Points. Home to the Green Turtle Lodge eco-resort. Very few crowds, ideal for relaxation.

name: Princess Town Beach
address: Princess Town, Ahanta West, Western Region
description: Quiet fishing village beach with the historic Fort Gross Friedrichsburg standing on the headland.

name: Fort St. Antonio
address: Axim Bay, Nzema East, Western Region
description: Built by the Portuguese in 1515, Fort St. Antonio is the second-oldest European fort in Ghana. Now a UNESCO World Heritage Site, it overlooks Axim Bay.
verified_source: UNESCO World Heritage; Ghana Museums and Monuments Board

name: Fort Metal Cross
description: British-built fort completed in 1697 in Dixcove. Renowned for its commanding views of the harbour and for its role in the Atlantic slave trade. UNESCO World Heritage Site.
verified_source: UNESCO; Ghana Museums and Monuments Board

name: Fort Batenstein
description: Dutch fort built in 1656 on a hill overlooking Butre village and lagoon. Now in ruins but with stunning views over the river mouth and beach.

name: Fort Orange (Fort Oranje)
description: Dutch fort built in 1640 in Sekondi, currently used as a lighthouse. UNESCO World Heritage Site.
verified_source: UNESCO; Ghana Museums and Monuments Board

name: Fort Gross Friedrichsburg
address: Princess Town (Pokesu), Ahanta West, Western Region
description: Brandenburg-Prussian fort built in 1683 — the only German-built fort in Ghana. Now operates as a guesthouse with sea views.
verified_source: UNESCO; Ghana Museums and Monuments Board

name: Fort Dorothea
description: Brandenburger fort built in 1685 near Akwidaa. Now in ruins, with the foundations and outer walls still visible.

name: Nzulezu Stilt Village
address: Lake Tadane, near Beyin, Jomoro District, Western Region
hours: Daily 8am–5pm; canoe trips depart Beyin visitor centre
description: A 400-year-old village built entirely on stilts above Lake Tadane. UNESCO tentative World Heritage Site. Reached by a one-hour canoe ride from Beyin through the Amansuri wetland.

name: Ankasa Conservation Area
description: 509 sq km of pristine rainforest — Ghana's most biodiverse protected area. Home to forest elephants, bongos, monkeys, and over 600 butterfly species.

name: Amansuri Wetland
description: Ramsar-designated wetland — the largest inland swamp forest in Ghana. Hosts crocodiles, herons, kingfishers, and is a key bird area.

name: Cape Three Points Lighthouse
address: Cape Three Points, Ahanta West, Western Region
description: Active lighthouse marking the southernmost point of Ghana. Built in 1925, it commands dramatic Atlantic views from a 56-metre cliff.

name: Bia National Park
address: Sefwi-Wiawso, Western North Region (border with Western Region)
description: Biosphere reserve and national park with semi-deciduous forest. Home to forest elephants, chimpanzees, and several monkey species.

name: Best Western Plus Atlantic Hotel
description: Four-star international-chain hotel in central Takoradi. Pool, fitness centre, conference rooms, on-site restaurant. Popular with oil industry executives.

name: Raybow International Hotel
address: Cape Coast Road, Takoradi, Western Region
description: Long-established three-star hotel in Takoradi with a swimming pool, restaurant, bar, and conference facilities.

name: Planters Lodge
description: Boutique colonial-style hotel set in lush gardens near the airport. Renowned for its restaurant, pool, and tranquil atmosphere.

name: Africa Beach Hotel
description: Beachfront hotel with direct ocean views, restaurant, and event hall. Popular for weddings and corporate retreats.

name: Green Turtle Lodge
address: Akwidaa Beach, near Cape Three Points, Western Region
description: Eco-friendly beachfront lodge with thatched bungalows. Solar-powered, vegetarian-leaning kitchen, sea turtle conservation programme.

name: Busua Inn
address: Busua Beach, Ahanta West, Western Region
description: Charming small beachfront inn at Busua, with a celebrated restaurant serving fresh seafood and continental dishes.

name: Lou Moon Lodge
address: Axim coastline, Nzema East, Western Region
description: Luxury private peninsula resort near Axim with chalets, infinity pool, private beach, and fine-dining restaurant.

name: Ankobra Beach Resort
address: Ankobra River mouth, near Axim, Western Region
description: Beach resort at the mouth of the Ankobra River, with thatched chalets, restaurant, and access to the lagoon and beach.

name: Captain Hook's Restaurant
description: Popular seafood restaurant in Takoradi specialising in grilled tilapia, lobster, prawns, and fresh fish straight from the harbour. Pepper soup highly recommended.

name: Aqua Safari Restaurant
description: Mid-range restaurant in Takoradi serving Ghanaian classics and continental dishes. Known for jollof rice, banku with tilapia, and grilled chicken.

name: Sir Max Hotel Restaurant
description: Hotel restaurant well known for traditional fufu with light soup and groundnut soup, palmnut soup, and waakye on weekends.

name: Daa Maame Chop Bar
description: Authentic local chop bar near Market Circle. Famous for fufu with light soup or groundnut soup, banku with okro stew, and tilapia. Cash only.

name: Auntie Akua's Waakye Joint
description: Roadside waakye specialist serving generous portions with shito, gari, boiled egg, and wele. One of Takoradi's most-loved breakfast spots.

name: Maa Adwoa Banku Spot
description: Local spot known for banku with grilled tilapia and pepper, kenkey with fish, and okro stew. Quintessential Fante coastal cuisine.

name: Barbeque Spot Tarkwa
description: Evening grill spot popular with mining staff. Specialises in khebab, grilled tilapia, and roasted plantain with kelewele.

name: Coconut Grove Beach Restaurant
address: Busua Beach, Ahanta West, Western Region
description: Beachfront restaurant at Busua serving seafood, pizza, and pasta with sea views. Popular with surfers and weekend visitors from Takoradi.

name: Kofi Brokeman Spot
description: Street stall famous for roasted plantain with groundnuts (kofi brokeman), kelewele, and grilled corn. Quick local snack.

name: Gold Fields Ghana — Tarkwa Mine
description: One of the world's largest open-pit gold mines, in operation since 1961. Major employer in the Tarkwa area.

name: Ghana Manganese Company — Nsuta Mine
description: Ghana's only producing manganese mine, operational since 1916. Located near Tarkwa.

name: Golden Star Wassa Gold Mine
description: Underground gold mine in the Wassa area, operated by Golden Star Resources / Chifeng.

name: GCB Bank — Takoradi Main Branch
description: Main GCB Bank branch in Takoradi. Full retail and commercial banking, ATM available 24/7.

name: Ecobank — Takoradi Market Circle
description: Ecobank branch in central Takoradi. Forex services, ATM, business and personal accounts.

name: Absa Bank — Takoradi
description: Absa (formerly Barclays) branch in Takoradi. Retail and corporate banking, ATM, forex.

name: Stanbic Bank — Takoradi
description: Stanbic Bank branch in Takoradi. Strong corporate banking presence, especially for oil & gas.

name: Zenith Bank — Tarkwa
description: Zenith Bank branch in Tarkwa serving the mining belt, with ATM and forex services.

name: Kundum Festival
address: Ahanta and Nzema traditional areas, Western Region
description: Major harvest festival of the Ahanta and Nzema people, celebrated in cycles starting from Lower Axim and rotating through coastal towns. Drumming, dancing, durbar of chiefs.
verified_source: Ghana Tourism Authority; Centre for National Culture

name: Ahobaa Festival
description: Festival of remembrance and purification celebrated by some Ahanta and Nzema communities. Marks the start of the year's spiritual cycle.

name: Takoradi Market Circle
address: Market Circle, Central Takoradi, Western Region
description: The largest market in the Western Region, in the heart of Takoradi. Sells fresh produce, fish, textiles (Akosombo wax prints), kente, cosmetics, and household goods.

name: Takoradi Mall
description: Modern shopping mall with Shoprite, banks, ATMs, fast food (KFC, Pizza Hut), cinemas, and fashion retailers.

name: Tarkwa Central Market
description: Main market in Tarkwa with fresh produce, prepared food, household goods, and electronics.

name: Western Regional Police Headquarters
description: Regional headquarters of the Ghana Police Service for the Western Region. Coordinates all divisional and district police commands.

name: Takoradi Central Police Station
description: Main police station in Takoradi, handling reports, investigations, and providing emergency response in the metropolis.

name: Mama's Kitchen Chop Bar
description: Beloved local chop bar famous for fufu with palm nut soup, light soup, and grasscutter. A must-visit for authentic Ghanaian flavours in Takoradi.

name: Sir Max Chop Bar
description: Popular spot for fufu with light soup, fish, and traditional Akan dishes. Clean environment with both indoor and outdoor seating.

name: Chop Bar Mansion
description: Tarkwa's go-to chop bar for fufu, light soup, palm nut soup, banku with tilapia, and waakye. Lively atmosphere especially at lunch.

name: Ahanta Bukom Restaurant
description: Specialises in Ahanta-style cuisine: fufu with light soup, akple with okro, and abenkwan (palm soup). Reasonable prices.

name: Auntie Ama's Banku Joint
description: Famous for banku with tilapia and pepper sauce, also serves kenkey and fried fish. A neighbourhood favourite for over a decade.

name: Jollof Junction
description: Specialty jollof rice spot — chicken jollof, beef jollof, and seafood jollof, with shito and salad. Quick lunch favourite.

name: Waakye Lady – Market Circle
description: Iconic morning waakye spot — rice and beans served with shito, gari, fried plantain, boiled egg, and choice of meat or fish.

name: Kenkey & Fish House
description: Fante kenkey with grilled tilapia, fried fish, and pepper sauce, served fresh from the nearby fishing harbour.

name: Akpeteshie & Pepper Soup Bar
description: Evening spot for goat pepper soup, grilled tilapia, kebabs, and akpeteshie. Lively bar atmosphere.

name: Coastal Grill Garden
description: Open-air beach restaurant offering fresh grilled lobster, prawns, fish, and continental options alongside Ghanaian classics.

name: Big Milly's Kitchen
description: Backpacker-friendly kitchen serving banku with tilapia, yam chips, omelettes, and fruit smoothies.

name: Tarkwa Royal Restaurant
description: Hotel-style restaurant — Ghanaian and continental menu including jollof, fried rice, fufu with light soup, and pizza.

name: Oma's Kitchen
description: Cozy spot in Axim serving Nzema-style banku with okro, fufu with palm soup, and fresh grilled fish from the bay.

name: Atlantic Hotel Takoradi
description: One of Takoradi's oldest hotels with sea views, swimming pool, restaurant, and conference facilities.

name: Best Western Plus Atlantic
description: International-brand business hotel with executive rooms, gym, restaurant, and meeting rooms.

name: Hillcrest Hotel Tarkwa
description: Comfortable mid-range hotel popular with mining sector visitors. Restaurant, bar, and pool.

name: Apremdo Polyclinic
description: Polyclinic offering general OPD, antenatal care, immunisation, and minor surgical procedures.

name: Adiembra Senior High School
description: Co-educational senior high school offering general arts, business, science, and home economics.

name: Ghana Secondary Technical School
description: One of Ghana's premier boys' boarding senior high schools, established 1909. Strong science and technical programmes.

name: St John's School Takoradi
description: Renowned Catholic boys' senior high school — strong academic record, sports tradition.

name: Methodist Day SHS Sekondi
description: Day senior high school under the Methodist Church — general arts, business, science.

name: GCB Bank Takoradi Main
description: Flagship branch of GCB Bank in Takoradi — full retail banking, business banking, ATM 24/7.

name: Ecobank Takoradi
description: Pan-African bank branch with full retail and SME services. ATM available 24/7.

name: Stanbic Bank Tarkwa
description: Stanbic Bank branch serving the mining region — corporate, retail, and forex services.

name: Fidelity Bank Sekondi
description: Fidelity Bank branch in Sekondi — retail banking, mobile banking, ATM.

name: CalBank Takoradi
description: CalBank branch — retail banking, SME services, foreign exchange, ATM.

name: Fort San Antonio Axim
description: Portuguese-built 1515 fort, second-oldest in Ghana. UNESCO World Heritage site. Guided tours available.

name: Fort Apollonia Beyin
description: British-built 1768 fort, now a museum of Nzema culture. UNESCO World Heritage site.

name: Amansuri Wetlands
description: Largest freshwater wetland in Ghana — canoe trips through the Amansuri Lagoon, stilt village of Nzulezo nearby.

name: Hilla Drug Store
description: Well-stocked pharmacy — prescription medications, OTC drugs, baby products, and first aid supplies.

name: Top-Up Pharmacy Takoradi
description: Central pharmacy with extended hours — prescriptions, vaccinations, blood pressure checks.

name: Ernest Chemists Sekondi
description: Branch of major Ghanaian pharmacy chain — full range of medications, cosmetics, baby care.

name: GOIL Filling Station Takoradi
description: Major GOIL station with petrol, diesel, LPG, mini-mart, and ATM. Truck-friendly bays.

name: Total Energies Tarkwa
description: Total Energies station serving the mining corridor — petrol, diesel, lubricants, Bonjour mini-mart.

name: Shell Sekondi
description: Shell forecourt with Helix lubricants, V-Power fuel, Shell Select shop.

name: Takoradi Train Station
description: Historic terminus of the Western Line — passenger services to Tarkwa and freight to mining regions.

name: VIP Bus Terminal Takoradi
description: Long-distance VIP coach terminal — daily services to Accra, Kumasi, Cape Coast.

name: STC Sekondi Terminal
description: State Transport Corporation depot — intercity buses to Accra, Cape Coast, and northern regions.

name: Takoradi Central Market
description: Largest open-air market in the Western Region — fresh produce, fish, textiles, household goods.

name: Tarkwa Police Headquarters
description: Tarkwa-Nsuaem Municipal Police HQ — emergency response, criminal investigation, road traffic.

name: Axim Police Station
description: District police station — emergency response, complaints, statements.

name: Silverbird Cinemas Takoradi
description: Multiplex cinema in Takoradi Mall — Hollywood, Bollywood, and African film releases.

name: Vienna City Pub
description: Popular nightclub and pub — live DJs, dance floor, cocktails, lounge area.

name: St Bernadette's Catholic Cathedral
description: Catholic cathedral of the Sekondi-Takoradi Diocese — Sunday masses, weddings, choir performances.

name: Sekondi Central Mosque
description: Central mosque of Sekondi — five daily prayers, Friday Jumu'ah, Islamic education.

name: Takoradi Technical University
description: Technical university offering engineering, business, applied sciences, and hospitality programmes.
